title: celtis tournefortii var. glabrata
intro: Celtis tournefortii var. glabrata, also known as the desert hackberry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Celtis genus. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various medicinal properties, which include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The desert hackberry has been reported to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be made by combining the dried leaves and bark with hot water or other solvents.
